key_insight: A reconstructed discontinuous Galerkin formulation on unfitted meshes can be preconditioned using a lowest-order piecewise constant space, yielding mesh-, coefficient-, and interface-robust solver performance.
engineering_relevance: This enables accurate and efficient simulation of elliptic interface problems with complex geometries without requiring mesh conformity to the interface.
method: Build a high-order reconstructed discontinuous approximation from local least-squares fits with one DOF per element.
Modify the local reconstruction with constraints to improve stability near the interface and prove norm equivalence to the lowest-order space.
Use the piecewise constant space as a preconditioner, with multigrid acting as the inverse of the lowest-order system.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments in 2D and 3D report optimal convergence and show the preconditioner remains effective independent of mesh size, coefficients, and interface position.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the local reconstruction and the multigrid solve for the lowest-order system, which may require careful implementation on highly cut meshes.
paper_title: Preconditioned Discontinuous Galerkin Method for Elliptic Interface Problem on Unfitted Mesh with Reconstructed Discontinuous Approximation
